name: With regard to matters such as these the Qur'an resorts to metaphors and figurative
introduction:
language. Hence it instructs men not to approach women. This does not mean that people
should either abstain from sitting together on the same floor or eating together when a
woman has her monthly period making her virtually an untouchable, as has been the
custom among the Jews, Hindus and certain other nations. The explanation of this
injunction by the Prophet makes it clear that during this period men are only required to
abstain from sexual intercourse; no change is postulated in other relationships, and the
woman is to be treated in the normal way. (See, for instance, Bukhari, 'Hayd', 10, 'Itikaf', 2-4,
10, 19; Muslim, 'Hayd', 6-13; Abu Da'ud, 'Taharah', 102, 106 - Ed.)
